Off Calendar Mar-03-2015 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Change Of Venue To Los Angeles County
Off Calendar Dec-16-2014 Continued To Mar-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Transfer Venue To Contra Costa County
Off Calendar Nov-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susan Gibbs for Wells Fargo Bank.

Ms. Gibbs stated that she wanted to file an additional declaration, that the declaration addresses the attorney's fees, and that she had submitted a revised proposed order this morning for the Court. In addition, Ms. Gibbs stated that she had used initials in her paperwork in lieu of full names and that entries such as "JH" were initials for "Jim Hyke/Hike". In addition, Ms. Gibbs argued in support of her fees, stating that she did not speak with the Great-Grandmother, that as for invoice notes/time entries that it was time consulting with Jim Hyke/Hike which was a benefit, that the 9/30/14 date was time for attending a hearing, that she billed for time spent notifying clients of the hearing outcome & time spent on preparing documents after the hearing/order of the Court, she addressed 1/10 of an hour billed, and stated that the 2/10 of an hour that was billed was for time spent calling the Examiner/emailing/efiling the petition. In closing, she stated that she reduced the fees by .6 hours because of the items that were objected to. At the conclusion of Ms. Gibbs argument, the Court took the matters under submission. The Court noted that it would approve the petition for the change of venue to Los Angeles and that the order would be all inclusive. Status on the change of venue was set for 12/16/14 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition To Transfer Venue To Contra Costa County
Off Calendar Sep-30-2014 Continued To Nov-04-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susan Gibbs for Wells Fargo Bank (Petitioner).

Ms. Gibbs stated that she also represents the grandmother and the trust advisor. The Court instructed counsel to file an amended petition to change venue to Los Angeles County and give notice. The Petition to Transfer Venue is continued to November 4, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the amended petition.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy McConico. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Sep-09-2014 Off Calendar

Petition For An Order Establishing Demorea Ferdinand Special Needs Trust; Authorizing Investment In
Off Calendar Jun-17-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susan Gibbs for and with Delores Lee (Guardian Ad Litem). Susan Jackson (Trustee) in Propria Persona. Robert Lee Investment Advisor from Wells Fargo Bank.

Ms. Jackson stated that she is located in Pasadena, California. Mr. Lee also stated that he is at the same location (Pasadena, CA). The Court noted that venue will be an issue going forward. Ms. Gibbs stated that the funds are with the Mitchell Law Group in their trust account, that it is a structured settlement and will not be funded until the Special Needs Trust is approved, that they have an IEP in place and that they are looking into speech therapy. The Court encouraged Ms. Gibbs to make a plan for the future, stated that it does not ordinarily give approval to purchase a home or a van, spoke of issues with regard to how title would be held, who will pay the mortgage and insurance, etc. Mr. Lee stated that they typically would hire a third-party sub-advisor for a Special Needs Trust but that they do not have to hire them, that everything is being managed in house and that they make it a point not to use proprietary funds in Special Needs Trusts. Ms. Jackson stated that they typically do not charge extraordinary fees. The Petition for An Order Establishing Special Needs Trust, etc. was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
